column8: In the year 122 AD, the Roman Emperor Hadrian (visit) _____ his provinces in Britain.
column9: On his visit, the Roman soldiers (tell) _____ him that Pictish tribes from Britain's north (attack) _____ them.
column10: So Hadrian (give) _____ the order to build a protective wall across one of the narrowest parts of the country.
column11: After 6 years of hard work, the Wall (finish) _____ in 128.
column12: It (be) _____ 117 kilometres long and about 4 metres high.
column14: Every 8 kilometres there (be) _____ a large fort in which up to 1,000 soldiers (find) _____ shelter.
column15: The soldiers (watch) _____ over the frontier to the north and (check) _____ the people who (want) _____ to enter or leave Roman Britain.
column16: In order to pass through the Wall, people (have to go) _____ to one of the small forts that (serve) _____ as gateways.
column17: Those forts (call) _____ milecastles because the distance from one fort to another (be) _____ one Roman mile (about 1,500 metres).
